exercises.title: Temperature with Properties
exercises.description: Create a Temperature class using @property for celsius with validation, and computed read-only properties for fahrenheit and kelvin.
hints: @celsius.setter def celsius(self, value): if value < -273.15: raise ValueError(...)

exercises.title: Product with Computed Price Property
exercises.description: Create a Product class with a validated price property and a discount property. Add a computed final_price read-only property.
exercises.expectedOutput:
29.99
Python Book: $29.99 -> $23.99 (20% off)
Price must be positive
Discount must be between 0 and 0.5
hints: @price.setter: if value <= 0: raise ValueError('Price must be positive')
@discount.setter: if not (0 <= value <= 0.5): raise ValueError('Discount must be between 0 and 0.5')

exercises.title: Read-Only Properties and Name Mangling
exercises.description: Create a BankAccount with a read-only balance property (no setter), a protected owner, and demonstrate name mangling with a private account number.
hints: @property def balance(self): return self._balance (no setter defined)
Without a setter, assigning to acc.balance raises AttributeError

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Property vs Direct Attribute
warmup.description: What does this code print? Notice how @property makes the method look like an attribute.
